---
name: Figure or animation
about: Request a new figure or animation, or an enhancement to an existing one (static or animated, bound to a paper / README / slide)
labels: enhancement, figure
---

## Figure or animation request

> *Checkbox tip:* `[ ]` = unchecked, `[x]` = checked.  Click the box in GitHub's rendered view to toggle, or hand-edit the markdown (copy `[x]` from here to paste over any `[ ]`).

This template covers **static figures** (PNG / PDF / SVG) and
**animations** (GIF / MP4 / WebM) bound to a paper, README, or slide
deck.

If the artefact is a **standalone research object** (interactive,
explorable, hosted in its own right -- e.g. a 3D viewer, an HTML
page with embedded JS, an explorable notebook), use the
[Research artifact](./research-artifact.md) template instead.

**Artefact type** (check one)

- [ ] static figure (single frame)
- [ ] animation (multi-frame, time-evolved)

**Affected repository** (check one)

- [ ] `dcl` -- Paper I
- [ ] `dcl-sm-derivation` -- Paper II
- [ ] `dcl-generator-zoo`
- [ ] `dcl-paper-03-tidal-ionization` -- Paper III
- [ ] `dcl-core`
- [ ] this meta / project repo
- [ ] other: _______________________

**Purpose**

What the figure / animation is supposed to show.

**Target location** (check any that apply)

- [ ] paper section / appendix (which?): _______________________
- [ ] README
- [ ] presentation / talk
- [ ] social post
- [ ] notes / documentation
- [ ] other: _______________________

**Source data / inputs**

Data, model output, or source material (e.g. `data/exp_NN.npy`,
`data/exp_NN_trajectory.npy`, a sympy expression, a hand-drawn
sketch).

**Style / dimensions** (static figures and animations)

Styling, dimensions, layout, colour constraints (e.g. paper-figure
size, screen-readable, print-friendly), formatting.

**Duration / FPS** (animations only)

Desired duration and frame rate.

**Output format** (check one or more)

*Static figure formats:*

- [ ] PNG (raster, for slides / web)
- [ ] PDF (vector, for paper)
- [ ] SVG (vector, for web / editable)

*Animation formats:*

- [ ] GIF (small / shareable)
- [ ] MP4 (presentation-quality)
- [ ] WebM

*Other:*

- [ ] interactive (3D / WebGL / explorable) -- *use the
      Research artifact template instead*
- [ ] other: _______________________

**Dependencies / related issues**

List dependencies and related issues.

**Success criteria**

Describe what success looks like.

**Additional context**

Any other context.
